name: Topological Trim (Leaf Peeling)
summary: The MHT roots are the tree's centre. Peel away all current leaves layer by layer; whatever survives when 2 or fewer nodes remain is the centre.
visualizationIntent: init: draw the whole tree, compute every node's degree, and collect the degree-1 nodes as the first leaf layer (frontier). loop: while more than 2 nodes remain, trim the entire current leaf layer. move/update: for each leaf, mark it trimmed (dimmed) and lower each surviving neighbour's degree; a neighbour that drops to degree 1 joins the next leaf layer. return: the 1 or 2 surviving nodes flash as the MHT roots.

lineExplanations.3: Start the list of graph nodes for the visual.
lineExplanations.5: Add a node with its id and label.
lineExplanations.6: Start the list of graph edges for the visual.
lineExplanations.8: Read one endpoint of the edge.
lineExplanations.10: Add the edge to the drawing (it stays visible the whole run).
lineExplanations.12: A tree of 1 or 2 nodes is its own centre.
lineExplanations.14: Every node is a root in this tiny tree.
lineExplanations.16: Build the adjacency list — who each node connects to.
lineExplanations.18: Give each node an empty neighbour list.
lineExplanations.19: Build the degree array — how many neighbours each node has.
lineExplanations.22: Loop over every edge to fill adjacency and degree.
lineExplanations.29: Build the state tracker (0 active, 1 leaf, 2 trimmed).
lineExplanations.32: Collect the first layer of leaves.
lineExplanations.34: A node with exactly one neighbour is a leaf.
lineExplanations.35: Add it to the leaf layer.
lineExplanations.37: Count how many nodes are still in the tree.
lineExplanations.38: Placeholder for the leaf being trimmed right now.
lineExplanations.39: Keep peeling while more than 2 nodes remain.
lineExplanations.40: How many leaves are in the current layer.
lineExplanations.41: Removing this whole layer leaves this many nodes.
lineExplanations.42: Index within the current leaf layer.
lineExplanations.43: Process every leaf in this layer.
lineExplanations.44: Take the leaf at the front of the layer.
lineExplanations.46: Mark it the active leaf (the cursor).
lineExplanations.48: Zero its degree so it is never counted again.
lineExplanations.52: Only touch neighbours still in the tree.
lineExplanations.53: Cutting the leaf lowers the neighbour's degree by one.
lineExplanations.54: Did the neighbour just become a leaf (degree 1)?
lineExplanations.55: If so, it joins the NEXT leaf layer.
lineExplanations.58: The leaf is fully trimmed; clear the cursor.
lineExplanations.59: Move to the next leaf in this layer.
lineExplanations.60: Rebuild the result from the survivors.
lineExplanations.62: A node that was never trimmed is a centre.
lineExplanations.63: Collect it as an MHT root.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: is the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def findMinHeightTrees(self, n, edges): n nodes, edges is the list of connections.
syntaxExplanations.3: [] creates the node list for the graph visual.
syntaxExplanations.4: for i in range(n): loops over every node id.
syntaxExplanations.5: append a dict with the node's id and label.
syntaxExplanations.6: [] creates the edge list for the graph visual.
syntaxExplanations.7: for e in range(len(edges)): loops over each edge.
syntaxExplanations.10: append a {from, to} dict describing the edge.
syntaxExplanations.12: n <= 2 is true for a 1- or 2-node tree.
syntaxExplanations.13: for i in range(n): loops over the few nodes.
syntaxExplanations.14: result.append(i) adds each node as a root.
syntaxExplanations.16: [] will hold one neighbour-list per node.
syntaxExplanations.17: for i in range(n): loops over every node.
syntaxExplanations.18: adj.append([]) gives node i an empty neighbour list.
syntaxExplanations.20: for i in range(n): loops over every node.
syntaxExplanations.21: degree.append(0) starts each degree at zero.
syntaxExplanations.22: for e in range(len(edges)): visits every edge.
syntaxExplanations.25: adj[a].append(b) records b next to a.
syntaxExplanations.26: adj[b].append(a) records a next to b (undirected).
syntaxExplanations.27: degree[a] = degree[a] + 1 counts the edge for a.
syntaxExplanations.28: degree[b] = degree[b] + 1 counts the edge for b.
syntaxExplanations.30: for i in range(n): loops over every node.
syntaxExplanations.32: [] will hold the current layer of leaves.
syntaxExplanations.33: for i in range(n): checks every node.
syntaxExplanations.34: degree[i] == 1 means node i is a leaf.
syntaxExplanations.35: leaves.append(i) adds it to the layer.
syntaxExplanations.37: remaining = n counts nodes still in the tree.
syntaxExplanations.38: cur = -1 is a sentinel (no active leaf yet).
syntaxExplanations.39: while remaining > 2: keeps peeling until the centre is left.
syntaxExplanations.40: len(leaves) is the size of the current layer.
syntaxExplanations.41: remaining = remaining - count removes this whole layer.
syntaxExplanations.43: while k < count: processes each leaf once.
syntaxExplanations.44: leaves[0] is the leaf at the front.
syntaxExplanations.45: leaves.pop(0) removes it from the layer.
syntaxExplanations.46: cur = leaf marks it the active cursor.
syntaxExplanations.48: degree[leaf] = 0 takes it out of the counting.
syntaxExplanations.50: while j < len(adj[leaf]): visits each neighbour.
syntaxExplanations.53: degree[nb] = degree[nb] - 1 removes the cut edge.
syntaxExplanations.54: degree[nb] == 1 means the neighbour is now a leaf.
syntaxExplanations.55: leaves.append(nb) adds it to the next layer.
syntaxExplanations.57: j = j + 1 moves to the next neighbour.
syntaxExplanations.58: cur = -1 clears the cursor after trimming.
syntaxExplanations.59: k = k + 1 moves to the next leaf.
syntaxExplanations.61: for i in range(n): checks every node.
syntaxExplanations.62: state[i] != 2 is true for nodes never trimmed.
syntaxExplanations.64: return result hands back the MHT roots.
